# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for insecure deserialization and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any unsafe deserialization of untrusted data into executable objects, complex runtime state, or unsafe object graphs.
4. Replace unsafe deserialization with safer data-only formats and explicit validation.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require 'json'
require 'base64'
require 'openssl'

class SecureSessionStore
  SECRET_KEY = ENV.fetch('SESSION_SECRET_KEY') { SecureRandom.hex(32) }
  HMAC_ALGORITHM = 'SHA256'
  MAX_SESSION_SIZE = 4096
  ALLOWED_CLASSES = [String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass, Array, Hash].freeze

  def initialize(cookies)
    @cookies = cookies
  end

  def read
    raw = @cookies[:session]
    return {} unless raw.present?

    payload, signature = raw.split('--', 2)
    return {} unless payload && signature

    unless secure_compare(generate_hmac(payload), signature)
      Rails.logger.warn("Session tampering detected: invalid HMAC signature")
      destroy
      return {}
    end

    decoded = Base64.strict_decode64(payload)
    data = JSON.parse(decoded)

    unless data.is_a?(Hash)
      Rails.logger.warn("Session tampering detected: unexpected data structure")
      destroy
      return {}
    end

    validate_session_values(data)

    data
  rescue JSON::ParserError, ArgumentError => e
    Rails.logger.warn("Session deserialization failed: #{e.message}")
    destroy
    {}
  end

  def write(session_data)
    unless session_data.is_a?(Hash)
      raise ArgumentError, "Session data must be a Hash"
    end

    sanitized = sanitize_session_data(session_data)
    json_string = JSON.generate(sanitized)
    encoded = Base64.strict_encode64(json_string)
    signature = generate_hmac(encoded)
    signed_payload = "#{encoded}--#{signature}"

    if signed_payload.bytesize > MAX_SESSION_SIZE
      raise ArgumentError, "Session data exceeds maximum cookie size of #{MAX_SESSION_SIZE} bytes"
    end

    @cookies[:session] = {
      value: signed_payload,
      httponly: true,
      secure: Rails.env.production?,
      same_site: :lax
    }
  end

  def destroy
    @cookies.delete(:session)
  end

  def [](key)
    read[key.to_s]
  end

  def []=(key, value)
    data = read
    data[key.to_s] = value
    write(data)
  end

  def delete(key)
    data = read
    data.delete(key.to_s)
    write(data)
  end

  private

  def generate_hmac(data)
    OpenSSL::HMAC.hexdigest(HMAC_ALGORITHM, SECRET_KEY, data)
  end

  def secure_compare(a, b)
    return false unless a.bytesize == b.bytesize
    ActiveSupport::SecurityUtils.secure_compare(a, b)
  end

  def sanitize_session_data(data)
    data.each_with_object({}) do |(key, value), sanitized|
      sanitized_key = key.to_s
      sanitized[sanitized_key] = sanitize_value(value)
    end
  end

  def sanitize_value(value)
    case value
    when String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      value
    when Symbol
      value.to_s
    when Array
      value.map { |v| sanitize_value(v) }
    when Hash
      value.each_with_object({}) do |(k, v), h|
        h[k.to_s] = sanitize_value(v)
      end
    else
      raise ArgumentError, "Unsupported session value type: #{value.class}. " \
        "Only primitive types, Arrays, and Hashes are allowed."
    end
  end

  def validate_session_values(data)
    data.each do |key, value|
      validate_value_type(value)
    end
  end

  def validate_value_type(value)
    case value
    when String, Integer, Float, TrueClass, FalseClass, NilClass
      # Safe primitive types from JSON parsing
    when Array
      value.each { |v| validate_value_type(v) }
    when Hash
      value.each do |k, v|
        validate_value_type(k)
        validate_value_type(v)
      end
    else
      raise ArgumentError, "Unexpected deserialized type: #{value.class}"
    end
  end
end

# Rails middleware integration
class SecureSessionMiddleware
  def initialize(app)
    @app = app
  end

  def call(env)
    request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    env['rack.session.store'] = SecureSessionStore.new(request.cookie_jar)
    @app.call(env)
  end
end

# Controller concern for easy access
module SessionManagement
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  private

  def secure_session
    @secure_session ||= SecureSessionStore.new(cookies)
  end
end
